name: Lanza's Lumaconi
recipeIngredient: 3/4 large onion, cut into 1/4 inch dice
2 pounds ground beef, cooked as directed
1 teaspoon black pepper, or to taste
1 10 oz package frozen chopped spinach, well drained
48 ounces favorite meat sauce or marinara
12 ounces mozzarella or Monterey Jack cheese, shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook pasta shells in salted boiling water until slightly firm in the center but still soft enough to handle (al dente). Set aside.
2. Heat olive oil in a large sauté pan. Sauté the garlic in the oil until golden brown.
3. Add onions and cook until they begin to brown. Then add ground beef and cook until fully cooked.
4. Turn off the heat and tilt the pan to allow fat to be collected with a ladle. Remove the excess fat, but keep the clear natural meat juices.
5. Add black pepper and then eggs. Heat through, stirring to mix thoroughly.
6. Cook until the eggs begin to set. Remove from heat and add Parmesan cheese and spinach. Mix well.
7. Stuff shells with the mixture.
8. To serve, arrange the desired number of stuffed shells in a casserole dish (a normal dinner portion is four to five shells per person). Top with a generous amount of meat or marinara sauce, then shred mozzarella or Monterey Jack cheese on top.
9. Bake at 325°F for 45 minutes, or until the center of the stuffed shell is hot.
